response.justification: Aisha Okonkwo's background is overwhelmingly in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang-based full-stack and systems work, with no demonstrated experience in Kotlin, Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or JVM tooling. While Java appears briefly in two older roles (Altom, NI LabWindows/CVI), it was peripheral and non-JVM-tooling in nature. The candidate has no evidence of IDE internals work, enterprise Java framework experience, or application server integration — all of which are core to this position. The profile is a strong mismatch for this specific technical domain.
strengths: Demonstrated software craftsmanship discipline (TDD, pairing, trunk-based development) that transfers well to quality-focused engineering teams
Brief Java exposure at Altom Consulting and National Instruments shows familiarity with the language at a surface level
Experience building developer tooling (MCP frameworks, test runners, fluent-assert libraries) shows relevant meta-level interest
concerns: No Kotlin experience anywhere in the resume — a core required skill for this role
Zero exposure to Spring, Quarkus, or Hibernate; enterprise Java frameworks are entirely absent from the profile
Primary technical identity is TypeScript/Node.js; JVM ecosystem depth and IDE internals experience are both missing

key_factors.factor: No IDE internals or JVM tooling work
